title: Cost tracking & token budget allocation (Coinmaster/Purse/Tithe)
body:
# Cost tracking & token budget allocation (Coinmaster/Purse/Tithe)

_Vocabulary bookmark from `docs/future/guild-vocabulary.md` (2026-04-10) — Coinmaster / Purse / Tithe._

## Goal

Establish first-class cost tracking and token budget allocation in the guild — a way to set per-commission budgets, see actual vs. projected spend, distinguish "cost of implementation" from "cost of coordination," and stop runaway agents before they bleed the treasury. The vocabulary tome names this cluster Coinmaster (the role that tracks balances), Purse (a per-commission token allocation), and Tithe (the visible overhead of coordination).

## Status

Parked — vocabulary bookmark, no work scheduled. Real gap, real history (cost-investigation session 023683e5 already burned ~$836 of context exploring the problem from the instrument-cost angle).

## Next Steps

If this becomes pressing — most likely trigger is another runaway-cost incident, or wanting hard per-commission budgets enforced rather than reported — expand this bookmark into a proper inquiry. Start by enumerating the data we already have: instrument JSON envelopes capture per-call usage (cost, tokens, duration), but there's no aggregation layer above that. The first useful artifact would be a per-commission rollup that adds up every session under that commission and shows total spend with implementation/overhead split.

## Context

**The vocabulary** (from the future-vocabulary tome):

- **Coinmaster** — tracks AI token balances and expenditures. Provides cost visibility and may participate in resource allocation decisions (e.g., which petitions to grant). *Real need, not blocking anything yet.*
- **Purse** — a token budget allocated to a specific commission or member. The Coinmaster manages the guild's overall treasury; purses are the parceled-out allocations. A commission gets a purse when dispatched. If the purse runs dry, the commission must stop or request more funds. *System mapping: token budget limits per agent invocation. This is a real operational need — runaway agents are expensive. The purse makes cost containment part of the metaphor rather than an afterthought.*
- **Tithe** — a portion of resources contributed back to the guild from each commission. Not every token spent on a commission goes to implementation — some overhead goes to sage consultation, engine operation, record-keeping. The tithe is that overhead, made visible. *Useful for understanding system efficiency.*

**Why this isn't just "add a budget cap":** the value of the framing is that it separates three orthogonal concerns —

1. *Visibility* (Coinmaster) — can we see what we're spending and where?
2. *Allocation* (Purse) — can we set a per-commission ceiling that stops the work when hit?
3. *Decomposition* (Tithe) — can we tell coordination overhead apart from real implementation cost?

Today we have partial answers to (1) at the call level (instrument JSON envelopes) and almost nothing for (2) and (3). The real question to settle when this wakes up is how much of this we want — full Coinmaster apparatus, or just budget caps in the Spider, or just better per-commission rollups in Laboratory.

**What we already know from the cost-investigation session:**

- Instrument runs were the dominant cost (~$368 of ~$836 total in 60 hours) due to cache-prefix misalignment. T4.1 holds the design that fixes that specific case.
- Per-call cost is captured via `claude --print --output-format json` envelopes; the runner now writes them to `instruments/<name>/runs/run-N.json`.
- Aggregate cost lives in `result.yaml` per instrument run.
- No equivalent capture exists for *commission-side* sessions (the implementation rigs themselves) — that's the biggest visibility gap.

**Cross-links into the active quest set:**

- T4.1 (unified instrument context) — same problem domain, narrower scope. Solves a specific cost pathology; doesn't add cost tracking infrastructure.
- T4 (X013 instrumentation review) — could absorb a "per-commission cost rollup" sub-question if the need surfaces before this bookmark wakes up.

## References

- Source tome: `docs/future/guild-vocabulary.md` § "Economics & Resources" (Purse, Tithe) and § "Roles" (Coinmaster)
- Cost-investigation session: `023683e5-bf03-4fe4-820c-fae9dd2fad7a`
- Existing capture: `packages/instruments/src/execute.ts`, `instruments/<name>/runs/run-N.json`
- Cross-link: T4.1 unified instrument context (`w-mnszh0do-132d76a70528`)
- Cross-link: T4 X013 instrumentation review (`w-mnszgv1h-5890bcecc2e3`)

## Notes

- The Coinmaster vocabulary cluster also implies *Bounty* (priority/tier system affecting dispatch) and *Levy* (mandatory contribution from houses to patron-priority work). Both are downstream of having Purse first — bookmark them only if/when this quest activates.
- 2026-04-10: opened as a vocabulary bookmark from the future-vocabulary tome.
